description_long: This object is a vintage Commodore PET 2001 Series personal computer, featuring an integrated monitor and keyboard in a single unit. The monitor is encased in a blue-gray bezel with a slightly angled display, showing a green monochrome graphical output. The lower section houses a wide, flat keyboard with a numeric keypad, function keys, and a distinctive layout, as well as a built-in cassette tape drive on the left. The body is primarily off-white with clean, angular lines and a robust, boxy form factor. The rear and sides are plain, with no visible ports or ventilation, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The front panel displays branding and model information, adding to its retro aesthetic.
description: A vintage Commodore PET computer with an integrated blue-gray monitor, off-white body, and a wide keyboard with a built-in tape drive.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-gray monitor with green display, full keyboard, tape drive, and branding.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the monitor and the protruding tape drive section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, off-white back with no visible features or ports.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the keyboard's angled layout and the solid, boxy structure.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, blue-gray, and angular with a matte finish, framing the display with straight edges and no visible decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is integrated into a flat, rectangular base that also houses the keyboard and tape drive, forming a single, solid unit with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are located on the keyboard area below the screen, grouped together on the left and right sides of the keyboard.
attribute_brand_logo: A Commodore logo and PET 2001 Series branding are present on the front panel below the screen, centered and clearly visible.
